parameters.subject: =Still Thinking It Over? Your Apex Windows Quote — ${{ $('Supabase SELECT — Fetch Quote & Accept Link').item.json.total_amount }}
parameters.message:
=

Hi {{ $('Supabase SELECT — Fetch Lead Details').item.json.name }},

Just a quick, friendly follow-up — we noticed you may not have had a chance to review your quote yet, and we wanted to make sure it didn't get buried in your inbox.

Your quote is still available, and you can view or accept it anytime using the button below:

View & Accept Your Quote

No rush at all — we're here whenever you're ready. If you have any questions, want to tweak anything, or just want to chat through your options, feel free to reply to this email or give us a call.

📞 (289) 546-9XXX

Best regards,
Apex Windows TX Team

nodes.name: Send Email — Node 21: Follow-Up to Customer

parameters.subject: =📋 Follow-Up Needed: {{ $('Supabase SELECT — Fetch Lead Details').item.json.name }} hasn't accepted quote ({{ $('Code — Calc Days Since Quote Sent1').item.json.days_since }} days)
parameters.message:
=

Hi,

This is an automated alert — a customer has not responded to their quote and a follow-up email has just been sent to them.

👤 Customer Details

Name:{{ $('Supabase SELECT — Fetch Lead Details').item.json.name }}
Email:{{ $('Supabase SELECT — Fetch Lead Details').item.json.email }}

💰 Quote Details

Quote Total:${{ $('Supabase SELECT — Fetch Quote & Accept Link').item.json.total_amount }}
Quote Sent:{{ $('Code — Calc Days Since Quote Sent1').item.json.quote_sent_at }}
Days Since Sent:{{ $('Code — Calc Days Since Quote Sent1').item.json.days_since }} days
Notification #:{{ ($('Code — Calc Days Since Quote Sent1').item.json.notification_count || 0) + 1 }}

✅ Action Taken: A friendly follow-up email was just sent to the customer.

📞 Recommended Next Step: Consider reaching out with a personal phone call to help move this forward.

— Apex Windows TX Automation System

parameters.subject: [TEST] Update on Your Apex Windows Quote

values.content: =Write a single warm paragraph (2-3 sentences) from Apex Windows TX to a customer named {{ $('Supabase SELECT — Fetch Lead for Escalation').item.json.name }}. Their window replacement quote is taking a little longer than usual. Apologise briefly, reassure them it will be ready in the next 1-2 days. Friendly and professional. No placeholders.

parameters.subject: Overdue Alert

=

🚨 OVERDUE ACTION REQUIRED QUOTE APPROOVAL PENDING

ACTION REQUIRED IMMEDIATELY

View Lead in CRM

parameters.subject: ={{ $json.email }} [TEST] Apex Windows TX — Seasonal Tips to Protect Your Windows
parameters.message:
= Apex Windows TX Newsletter

Apex Windows TX

Seasonal Window Care Tips

Hi {{ $json.name }},

Hope everything is going great with your home!

Here are a few simple tips to keep your windows in great condition and avoid costly repairs:

  • If you notice fog or condensation between panes, the seal may have failed
  • Many foggy windows are still under IGU warranty — worth checking before replacing
  • Regular cleaning and inspection can extend window life

💡 Pro Tip: If your windows are starting to fog again, fixing them early can save you money.

🎁 Referral Offer

Refer someone with foggy windows and get

10% OFF your next service

If you need help or want us to take a quick look, just reply to this email or call us anytime.

📞 (+22)89582

Best regards,
Apex Windows TX Team

You’re receiving this because you worked with Apex Windows TX.

If you prefer not to receive these emails, you can unsubscribe anytime.

parameters.content:
# Find leads where quote is delayed >24hrs and not resolved
# Alert Sales Manager + send delay email to customer (if needed)

# Find customers who haven’t responded 72hrs after quote sent
# Send follow-up email + notify Sales Manager for action

# If review wasn’t sent after job completion, send it now
# Prevent duplicates using post_job_comms check

# Find completed customers who haven’t received newsletter in 90 days
# Send value email + update last_newsletter_sent_at to avoid repeats
